name: Mathematics
description: Mathematics covering counting, operations, place value, fractions, measurement, geometry, ratios, algebra, and statistics. Grades K-8.

topics.name: Count to 10
topics.description: Count objects from 1 to 10 with one-to-one correspondence.

topics.name: Count to 20
topics.description: Count objects from 1 to 20.

topics.name: Count to 100
topics.description: Count to 100 by ones and by tens.

topics.name: Compare Numbers (K)
topics.description: Compare two numbers between 1 and 10 using greater than, less than, equal to.

topics.name: Add Within 5
topics.description: Add two numbers whose sum is 5 or less using objects or drawings.

topics.name: Subtract Within 5
topics.description: Subtract within 5 using objects or drawings.

topics.name: Add Within 10
topics.description: Fluently add within 10.

topics.name: Subtract Within 10
topics.description: Fluently subtract within 10.

topics.name: Teen Numbers (Place Value)
topics.description: Understand teen numbers as ten ones and some more ones (10 + n).

topics.name: 2D Shapes (K)
topics.description: Identify and describe squares, circles, triangles, rectangles.

topics.name: 3D Shapes (K)
topics.description: Identify and describe cubes, cones, cylinders, spheres.

topics.name: Add Within 20
topics.description: Add within 20 using strategies like making ten and counting on.

topics.name: Subtract Within 20
topics.description: Subtract within 20 using strategies related to addition.

topics.name: Word Problems (Add/Sub within 20)
topics.description: Solve addition and subtraction word problems within 20.

topics.name: Place Value: Tens and Ones
topics.description: Understand two-digit numbers as tens and ones.

topics.name: Compare Two-Digit Numbers
topics.description: Compare two-digit numbers using >, <, = based on place value.

topics.name: Add Within 100
topics.description: Add within 100 using place value strategies.

topics.name: Measure Length (Nonstandard Units)
topics.description: Measure length using nonstandard units like paper clips.

topics.name: Tell Time (Hour and Half Hour)
topics.description: Tell and write time in hours and half-hours using analog and digital clocks.

topics.name: Skip Count by 2s, 5s, 10s
topics.description: Skip count by 2, 5, and 10 within 1000.

topics.name: Fluently Add Within 100
topics.description: Fluently add within 100 using strategies based on place value.

topics.name: Fluently Subtract Within 100
topics.description: Fluently subtract within 100 using strategies based on place value.

topics.name: Add Within 1000
topics.description: Add up to four two-digit numbers or add within 1000.

topics.name: Subtract Within 1000
topics.description: Subtract within 1000 using models and strategies.

topics.name: Place Value: Hundreds
topics.description: Understand three-digit numbers as hundreds, tens, and ones.

topics.name: Measure Length (Standard Units)
topics.description: Measure and estimate lengths in standard units (inches, feet, centimeters, meters).

topics.name: Money: Coins and Bills
topics.description: Solve word problems involving dollar bills, quarters, dimes, nickels, pennies.

topics.name: Introduction to Arrays
topics.description: Use arrays to understand repeated addition as a foundation for multiplication.

topics.name: Odd and Even Numbers
topics.description: Determine whether a group of objects has an odd or even number.

topics.name: Multiply Within 100
topics.description: Fluently multiply within 100 (multiplication facts to 10×10).

topics.name: Divide Within 100
topics.description: Fluently divide within 100 (division facts related to multiplication).

topics.name: Multiplication Word Problems
topics.description: Solve word problems involving equal groups, arrays, and comparison.

topics.name: Division Word Problems
topics.description: Solve word problems involving equal sharing and grouping.

topics.name: Properties of Multiplication
topics.description: Apply commutative, associative, and distributive properties of multiplication.

topics.name: Patterns in Arithmetic
topics.description: Identify arithmetic patterns (including patterns in the addition and multiplication tables).

topics.name: Round to Nearest 10 or 100
topics.description: Use place value to round whole numbers to the nearest 10 or 100.

topics.name: Multi-Digit Addition and Subtraction
topics.description: Fluently add and subtract within 1000 using algorithms.

topics.name: Introduction to Fractions
topics.description: Understand a fraction 1/b as one part when a whole is partitioned into b equal parts.

topics.name: Fractions on a Number Line
topics.description: Represent fractions on a number line.

topics.name: Equivalent Fractions (Intro)
topics.description: Understand and identify simple equivalent fractions (e.g., 1/2 = 2/4).

topics.name: Compare Fractions (Same Denominator/Numerator)
topics.description: Compare fractions with same numerator or same denominator.

topics.name: Perimeter
topics.description: Find the perimeter of polygons given side lengths. Solve perimeter word problems.

topics.name: Area (Introduction)
topics.description: Measure areas by counting unit squares. Understand area as covering.

topics.name: Area by Multiplying
topics.description: Find area of rectangles by multiplying side lengths.

topics.name: Tell Time to the Minute
topics.description: Tell and write time to the nearest minute. Solve elapsed time problems.

topics.name: Bar Graphs and Picture Graphs
topics.description: Draw and interpret scaled bar graphs and picture graphs.

topics.name: Multi-Digit Multiplication
topics.description: Multiply a whole number up to four digits by a one-digit number, and two two-digit numbers.

topics.name: Long Division
topics.description: Divide up to four-digit dividends by one-digit divisors.

topics.name: Factors and Multiples
topics.description: Find factor pairs, determine if a number is prime or composite.

topics.name: Equivalent Fractions
topics.description: Generate equivalent fractions using visual models and multiplication/division.

topics.name: Compare Fractions (Unlike Denominators)
topics.description: Compare fractions with unlike denominators by finding common denominators.

topics.name: Add and Subtract Fractions (Same Denominator)
topics.description: Add and subtract fractions with the same denominator.

topics.name: Multiply Fraction by Whole Number
topics.description: Multiply a fraction by a whole number (e.g., 3 × 2/5).

topics.name: Introduction to Decimals
topics.description: Understand decimal notation for fractions with denominators 10 and 100.

topics.name: Angles (Introduction)
topics.description: Understand angles as a measure of rotation. Measure angles with a protractor.

topics.name: Line Symmetry
topics.description: Identify line-symmetric figures and draw lines of symmetry.

topics.name: Multi-Step Word Problems
topics.description: Solve multi-step word problems using the four operations.

topics.name: Place Value with Decimals
topics.description: Understand the place value system extended to decimals (tenths, hundredths, thousandths).

topics.name: Multiply Multi-Digit Whole Numbers
topics.description: Fluently multiply multi-digit whole numbers using standard algorithm.

topics.name: Divide Multi-Digit Whole Numbers
topics.description: Divide whole numbers with up to four-digit dividends and two-digit divisors.

topics.name: Add/Subtract Fractions (Unlike Denominators)
topics.description: Add and subtract fractions with unlike denominators by finding common denominators.

topics.name: Multiply Fractions
topics.description: Multiply fractions and mixed numbers.

topics.name: Divide Fractions
topics.description: Divide unit fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by unit fractions.

topics.name: Decimal Operations
topics.description: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals to hundredths.

topics.name: Order of Operations
topics.description: Evaluate expressions with parentheses, brackets, and braces (PEMDAS).

topics.name: Variables and Expressions
topics.description: Write and evaluate simple expressions with variables.

topics.name: Coordinate Plane (Introduction)
topics.description: Graph points in the first quadrant of the coordinate plane.

topics.name: Volume
topics.description: Find volumes of right rectangular prisms. Use V = l × w × h.

topics.name: Classify 2D Shapes
topics.description: Classify triangles and quadrilaterals based on properties.

topics.name: Line Plots with Fractions
topics.description: Make line plots to display data measured in fractions.

topics.name: Unit Conversion
topics.description: Convert among different-sized measurement units within a system.

topics.name: Prime and Composite Numbers
topics.description: Determine whether a whole number is prime or composite by finding its factors.

topics.name: Greatest Common Factor
topics.description: Find the greatest common factor (GCF) of two whole numbers up to 100.

topics.name: Least Common Multiple
topics.description: Find the least common multiple (LCM) of two whole numbers up to 12.

topics.name: Improper Fractions and Mixed Numbers
topics.description: Convert between improper fractions and mixed numbers.

topics.name: Add and Subtract Mixed Numbers
topics.description: Add and subtract mixed numbers with like denominators, including regrouping.

topics.name: Add and Subtract Mixed Numbers (Unlike Denominators)
topics.description: Add and subtract mixed numbers with unlike denominators using equivalent fractions.

topics.name: Multiply Mixed Numbers
topics.description: Multiply mixed numbers by converting to improper fractions and simplifying.

topics.name: Fraction-Decimal Equivalence
topics.description: Convert between fractions and decimals, including common benchmark fractions.

topics.name: Compare Decimals
topics.description: Compare two decimals to hundredths using place value understanding and symbols <, >, =.

topics.name: Round Decimals
topics.description: Round decimals to any place value using place value understanding.

topics.name: Powers of Ten
topics.description: Explain patterns when multiplying or dividing by powers of 10, and use whole-number exponents to denote powers of 10.

topics.name: Exponents (Introduction)
topics.description: Evaluate numerical expressions involving whole-number exponents.

topics.name: Estimate Sums and Differences
topics.description: Use rounding and compatible numbers to estimate sums and differences.

topics.name: Estimate Products and Quotients
topics.description: Use rounding and compatible numbers to estimate products and quotients.

topics.name: Points, Lines, Rays, and Segments
topics.description: Identify and describe points, lines, line segments, and rays.

topics.name: Parallel and Perpendicular Lines
topics.description: Identify and draw parallel and perpendicular lines in two-dimensional figures.

topics.name: Classify Triangles
topics.description: Classify triangles by their side lengths (equilateral, isosceles, scalene) and angle measures (acute, right, obtuse).

topics.name: Area of Composite Shapes
topics.description: Find the area of composite figures by decomposing them into non-overlapping rectangles.

topics.name: Mean, Median, and Mode
topics.description: Find the mean, median, and mode of a data set and understand what each measure tells us.

topics.name: Number Patterns and Sequences
topics.description: Generate and analyze number patterns using given rules, and identify relationships between corresponding terms.

topics.name: Numerical Expressions with Grouping Symbols
topics.description: Evaluate numerical expressions containing parentheses, brackets, and braces.

topics.name: Introduction to Integers
topics.description: Understand positive and negative integers and their position on the number line.

topics.name: Absolute Value
topics.description: Understand absolute value as distance from zero. Evaluate |a| for integers and rationals.

topics.name: Compare and Order Integers
topics.description: Compare and order positive and negative integers using a number line and inequality symbols.

topics.name: Add Integers
topics.description: Add positive and negative integers using number line models and rules for same/different signs.

topics.name: Subtract Integers
topics.description: Subtract integers by adding the additive inverse. Understand p - q = p + (-q).

topics.name: Multiply Integers
topics.description: Multiply positive and negative integers. Understand sign rules for products.

topics.name: Divide Integers
topics.description: Divide positive and negative integers. Apply sign rules for quotients.

topics.name: Rational Numbers on the Number Line
topics.description: Represent fractions and decimals (positive and negative) on the number line. Understand rational numbers as a/b where b != 0.

topics.name: Compare and Order Rational Numbers
topics.description: Compare and order positive and negative fractions and decimals using common denominators and number lines.

topics.name: Add and Subtract Rational Numbers
topics.description: Add and subtract positive and negative fractions and decimals.

topics.name: Multiply and Divide Rational Numbers
topics.description: Multiply and divide positive and negative fractions and decimals.

topics.name: Convert Between Rational Number Forms
topics.description: Convert between fractions, decimals, and percents. Identify repeating vs terminating decimals.

topics.name: Multi-Step Rational Number Operations
topics.description: Solve multi-step problems involving all four operations with rational numbers.

topics.name: Opposites and Additive Inverse
topics.description: Understand that every number has an opposite and that a + (-a) = 0.

topics.name: GCF and LCM Applications
topics.description: Use GCF and LCM to solve real-world problems. Apply distributive property with GCF: a + b = GCF(a,b)(a/GCF + b/GCF).

topics.name: Fluent Multi-Digit Division
topics.description: Fluently divide multi-digit numbers using the standard algorithm.

topics.name: Divide Decimals
topics.description: Fluently add, subtract, multiply, and divide multi-digit decimals using standard algorithms.

topics.name: Square Roots
topics.description: Evaluate square roots of perfect squares. Estimate square roots of non-perfect squares between consecutive integers.

topics.name: Introduction to Ratios
topics.description: Understand the concept of a ratio. Write ratios in three forms (a:b, a to b, a/b).

topics.name: Equivalent Ratios
topics.description: Generate equivalent ratios using ratio tables and multiplication/division.

topics.name: Unit Rates
topics.description: Find and interpret unit rates (including those with complex fractions in grade 7).

topics.name: Ratio Tables
topics.description: Use ratio tables to solve problems. Find missing values in ratio tables.

topics.name: Proportions
topics.description: Determine if two ratios form a proportion. Solve proportions using cross multiplication.

topics.name: Proportional Relationships
topics.description: Identify proportional relationships in tables, graphs, and equations. Understand y = kx.

topics.name: Constant of Proportionality
topics.description: Identify the constant of proportionality (unit rate) in tables, graphs, equations, and verbal descriptions.

topics.name: Introduction to Percent
topics.description: Understand percent as a rate per 100. Convert between fractions, decimals, and percents.

topics.name: Percent of a Number
topics.description: Find a percent of a quantity. Solve problems like 'What is 25% of 80?'

topics.name: Percent Increase and Decrease
topics.description: Calculate percent increase and percent decrease. Solve markup, discount, and tip problems.

topics.name: Tax, Tip, and Markup
topics.description: Solve real-world problems involving tax, tip, markup, markdown, and commission.

topics.name: Simple Interest
topics.description: Calculate simple interest using I = Prt. Solve problems involving principal, rate, and time.

topics.name: Scale Drawings
topics.description: Solve problems involving scale drawings. Compute actual lengths and areas from scale factors.

topics.name: Unit Rates with Complex Fractions
topics.description: Compute unit rates involving complex fractions (e.g., 1/2 mile per 1/4 hour = 2 mph).

topics.name: Ratio and Rate Word Problems
topics.description: Solve real-world ratio and rate problems using tables, diagrams, and equations.

topics.name: Percent Equations
topics.description: Solve 'part = percent x whole' equations. Find the part, percent, or whole given the other two.

topics.name: Percent Error
topics.description: Calculate percent error as |measured - actual| / actual x 100.

topics.name: Write Algebraic Expressions
topics.description: Write algebraic expressions from verbal phrases. Identify parts of an expression (terms, coefficients, constants).

topics.name: Evaluate Algebraic Expressions
topics.description: Evaluate expressions at specific values of their variables, including expressions with exponents.

topics.name: Equivalent Expressions
topics.description: Generate equivalent expressions using properties of operations (commutative, associative, distributive).

topics.name: Distributive Property
topics.description: Apply the distributive property to expand and factor expressions: a(b + c) = ab + ac.

topics.name: One-Step Equations (Add/Subtract)
topics.description: Solve one-step equations using addition and subtraction: x + a = b, x - a = b.

topics.name: One-Step Equations (Multiply/Divide)
topics.description: Solve one-step equations using multiplication and division: ax = b, x/a = b.

topics.name: One-Step Inequalities
topics.description: Write and solve one-step inequalities. Graph solution sets on a number line.

topics.name: Two-Step Equations
topics.description: Solve equations of the form px + q = r and p(x + q) = r using inverse operations.

topics.name: Two-Step Inequalities
topics.description: Solve two-step inequalities. Understand flipping the inequality when multiplying/dividing by a negative.

topics.name: Combine Like Terms
topics.description: Simplify expressions by combining like terms. Identify like terms in multi-variable expressions.

topics.name: Expand and Factor Linear Expressions
topics.description: Use the distributive property to expand expressions and factor common factors from expressions.

topics.name: Multi-Step Equations
topics.description: Solve equations requiring distribution, combining like terms, and variables on both sides.

topics.name: Equations with Special Solutions
topics.description: Determine whether a linear equation has one solution, no solution, or infinitely many solutions.

topics.name: Dependent and Independent Variables
topics.description: Identify dependent and independent variables. Write equations to express one quantity in terms of another.

topics.name: Write and Solve Equations from Word Problems
topics.description: Translate word problems into one-step equations and solve them.

topics.name: Multi-Step Word Problems with Equations
topics.description: Translate complex word problems into two-step equations and inequalities, then solve.

topics.name: Exponent Rules
topics.description: Apply product rule, quotient rule, power rule, and zero/negative exponent rules.

topics.name: Negative Exponents
topics.description: Evaluate expressions with negative exponents. Understand a^(-n) = 1/a^n.

topics.name: Scientific Notation
topics.description: Express very large and very small numbers in scientific notation. Convert between standard and scientific notation.

topics.name: Operations with Scientific Notation
topics.description: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide numbers in scientific notation.

topics.name: Irrational Numbers
topics.description: Understand that irrational numbers cannot be expressed as fractions. Classify numbers as rational or irrational.

topics.name: Cube Roots
topics.description: Evaluate cube roots of perfect cubes. Understand the relationship between cubes and cube roots.

topics.name: Coordinate Plane (Four Quadrants)
topics.description: Plot and identify ordered pairs in all four quadrants. Reflect points across axes.

topics.name: Graph Proportional Relationships
topics.description: Graph proportional relationships on the coordinate plane. Identify the unit rate as the slope.

topics.name: Introduction to Slope
topics.description: Understand slope as rate of change (rise/run). Calculate slope from two points and from a graph.

topics.name: Types of Slope
topics.description: Identify positive, negative, zero, and undefined slopes. Relate slope direction to real-world contexts.

topics.name: Y-Intercept
topics.description: Identify the y-intercept from equations, tables, and graphs. Understand its meaning in context.

topics.name: Slope-Intercept Form (y = mx + b)
topics.description: Write linear equations in slope-intercept form. Identify slope and y-intercept from the equation.

topics.name: Graph Linear Equations
topics.description: Graph linear equations using slope and y-intercept, tables of values, or intercepts.

topics.name: Write Linear Equations
topics.description: Write linear equations from tables, graphs, two points, or verbal descriptions.

topics.name: Compare Linear Functions
topics.description: Compare properties (slope, y-intercept) of two linear functions given in different forms.

topics.name: Introduction to Functions
topics.description: Understand that a function assigns each input exactly one output. Determine if a relation is a function.

topics.name: Function Tables and Rules
topics.description: Complete function tables given a rule. Write rules from input-output tables.

topics.name: Linear vs Nonlinear Functions
topics.description: Distinguish linear from nonlinear functions using tables, graphs, and equations.

topics.name: Qualitative Graphs
topics.description: Describe functional relationships qualitatively from graphs (increasing/decreasing, linear/nonlinear).

topics.name: Systems of Equations (Introduction)
topics.description: Understand systems of two linear equations. Solve by graphing and identify the intersection point.

topics.name: Systems of Equations: Substitution
topics.description: Solve systems of linear equations algebraically using the substitution method.

topics.name: Systems of Equations: Elimination
topics.description: Solve systems of linear equations algebraically using the elimination (linear combination) method.

topics.name: Systems of Equations Word Problems
topics.description: Translate real-world problems into systems of two linear equations and solve them.

topics.name: Area of Triangles
topics.description: Find the area of triangles using A = 1/2 * b * h.

topics.name: Area of Quadrilaterals
topics.description: Find the area of parallelograms, trapezoids, and other special quadrilaterals.

topics.name: Area of Polygons
topics.description: Find the area of irregular polygons by decomposing into triangles and rectangles.

topics.name: Surface Area of Prisms
topics.description: Find the surface area of rectangular prisms and triangular prisms using nets.

topics.name: Volume of Prisms
topics.description: Find the volume of right rectangular prisms with fractional edge lengths. Use V = Bh.

topics.name: Angle Relationships
topics.description: Identify and use complementary, supplementary, vertical, and adjacent angle relationships.

topics.name: Triangle Angle Sum
topics.description: Understand and apply the triangle angle sum theorem (angles sum to 180 degrees).

topics.name: Exterior Angle Theorem
topics.description: Apply the exterior angle theorem for triangles: exterior angle = sum of remote interior angles.

topics.name: Parallel Lines and Transversals
topics.description: Identify and use angle relationships formed by parallel lines cut by a transversal (corresponding, alternate interior, etc.).

topics.name: Circumference of Circles
topics.description: Calculate the circumference of circles using C = pi*d and C = 2*pi*r.

topics.name: Area of Circles
topics.description: Calculate the area of circles using A = pi*r^2.

topics.name: Cross Sections of 3D Figures
topics.description: Describe the two-dimensional figure that results from slicing a three-dimensional figure.

topics.name: Volume of Cylinders
topics.description: Find the volume of cylinders using V = pi*r^2*h.

topics.name: Volume of Cones and Spheres
topics.description: Find the volume of cones (V = 1/3*pi*r^2*h) and spheres (V = 4/3*pi*r^3).

topics.name: Pythagorean Theorem
topics.description: Understand and apply the Pythagorean theorem: a^2 + b^2 = c^2.

topics.name: Converse of the Pythagorean Theorem
topics.description: Use the converse of the Pythagorean theorem to determine if a triangle is a right triangle.

topics.name: Pythagorean Theorem Applications
topics.description: Apply the Pythagorean theorem to find distances in coordinate planes and real-world 2D/3D problems.

topics.name: Transformations: Translations, Reflections, Rotations
topics.description: Describe and perform translations, reflections, and rotations on the coordinate plane.

topics.name: Dilations
topics.description: Perform dilations with a given center and scale factor. Understand effect on coordinates.

topics.name: Congruence and Similarity
topics.description: Understand congruence via rigid motions and similarity via dilations. Identify similar and congruent figures.

topics.name: Statistical Questions
topics.description: Distinguish statistical questions from non-statistical questions. Understand variability.

topics.name: Measures of Center
topics.description: Calculate mean, median, and mode for data sets. Choose the appropriate measure of center.

topics.name: Measures of Variability
topics.description: Calculate range, interquartile range (IQR), and mean absolute deviation (MAD).

topics.name: Dot Plots and Histograms
topics.description: Display and interpret data using dot plots and histograms. Describe shape and distribution.

topics.name: Box Plots
topics.description: Create and interpret box plots. Identify median, quartiles, and outliers.

topics.name: Summarize Data Distributions
topics.description: Summarize numerical data sets in relation to their context (shape, center, spread, outliers).

topics.name: Introduction to Probability
topics.description: Understand probability as a number between 0 and 1. Find probabilities of simple events.

topics.name: Experimental Probability
topics.description: Calculate experimental probability from repeated trials. Compare experimental to theoretical probability.

topics.name: Compound Probability
topics.description: Find probabilities of compound events using organized lists, tables, tree diagrams, and simulation.

topics.name: Sampling and Inference
topics.description: Understand random sampling. Use sample data to draw inferences about a population.

topics.name: Compare Two Populations
topics.description: Compare two data distributions using measures of center and variability. Draw informal inferences.

topics.name: Scatter Plots
topics.description: Construct and interpret scatter plots. Describe patterns (clustering, outliers, positive/negative association, linear/nonlinear).

topics.name: Line of Best Fit
topics.description: Informally fit a straight line to data in a scatter plot. Use the equation to make predictions.

topics.name: Two-Way Frequency Tables
topics.description: Construct and interpret two-way tables of categorical data. Compute relative frequencies.

topics.name: Monomials
topics.description: Identify monomials and their degree. Multiply and divide monomials using exponent rules.

topics.name: Classify Polynomials
topics.description: Identify polynomials by number of terms (monomial, binomial, trinomial) and degree. Write in standard form.

topics.name: Add and Subtract Polynomials
topics.description: Add and subtract polynomials by combining like terms.

topics.name: Multiply Polynomials by Monomials
topics.description: Multiply a polynomial by a monomial using the distributive property.

topics.name: Multiply Binomials (FOIL)
topics.description: Multiply two binomials using FOIL or the distributive property.

topics.name: Factor Out GCF from Polynomials
topics.description: Factor the greatest common factor from polynomial expressions.

topics.name: Special Products
topics.description: Recognize and apply patterns for (a+b)^2, (a-b)^2, and (a+b)(a-b).

topics.name: Count Objects to 5
topics.description: Count a set of up to 5 objects with one-to-one correspondence.

topics.name: Count Objects to 10
topics.description: Count a set of up to 10 objects, saying number names in order.

topics.name: Count Objects to 20
topics.description: Count a set of up to 20 objects arranged in various configurations.

topics.name: One-to-One Correspondence
topics.description: Match each object in a set to exactly one number when counting.

topics.name: Count Forward from a Given Number
topics.description: Start counting from any number within 20, not just from 1.

topics.name: Count Backward from 10
topics.description: Count backward from 10 to 0.

topics.name: Count Backward from 20
topics.description: Count backward from 20 to 0.

topics.name: Compare Groups (More/Less/Same)
topics.description: Compare two groups of objects and determine which has more, fewer, or if they are the same.

topics.name: Order Numbers to 10
topics.description: Arrange numbers from 0 to 10 in order from least to greatest or greatest to least.

topics.name: Order Numbers to 20
topics.description: Arrange numbers from 0 to 20 in order from least to greatest or greatest to least.

topics.name: Number Bonds to 10 (Compose/Decompose)
topics.description: Break apart and put together numbers within 10 (e.g., 7 = 3 + 4).

topics.name: Addition Doubles (1+1 through 10+10)
topics.description: Add doubles facts from 1+1 to 10+10 as a mental math strategy.

topics.name: Addition Near-Doubles
topics.description: Use doubles facts to add near-doubles (e.g., 6+7 = 6+6+1).

topics.name: Make Ten Strategy
topics.description: Decompose an addend to make a ten when adding (e.g., 8+5 = 8+2+3 = 10+3).

topics.name: Add Three Numbers (within 20)
topics.description: Add three single-digit or small numbers with a sum within 20.

topics.name: Add Two-Digit Numbers (No Regrouping)
topics.description: Add two two-digit numbers without regrouping (e.g., 32 + 15).

topics.name: Add Two-Digit Numbers (With Regrouping)
topics.description: Add two two-digit numbers with regrouping ones to tens (e.g., 38 + 47).

topics.name: Add Three-Digit Numbers
topics.description: Add three-digit numbers using the standard algorithm with regrouping.

topics.name: Add Multi-Digit Numbers (4+ digits)
topics.description: Add numbers with four or more digits using the standard algorithm.

topics.name: Add Three or More Multi-Digit Numbers
topics.description: Add three or more multi-digit numbers in a single problem.

topics.name: Estimate Sums
topics.description: Use rounding and compatible numbers to estimate sums.

topics.name: Properties of Addition (Commutative, Associative, Identity)
topics.description: Understand and apply commutative, associative, and identity properties of addition.

topics.name: Addition Word Problems: Join/Combine
topics.description: Solve word problems involving joining or combining quantities.

topics.name: Addition Word Problems: Compare
topics.description: Solve word problems involving comparison using addition (how many more/fewer).

topics.name: Multi-Step Addition Word Problems
topics.description: Solve word problems requiring two or more addition steps.

topics.name: Mental Addition Strategies (Compensation, Splitting)
topics.description: Use mental strategies like compensation (add 100, subtract 1) and splitting (break apart by place value) to add.

topics.name: Addition on a Number Line
topics.description: Represent and solve addition problems by making jumps on a number line.

topics.name: Missing Addend Problems
topics.description: Find the unknown addend in equations like 5 + ? = 12.

topics.name: Subtract by Counting Back
topics.description: Subtract small numbers by counting backward from the starting number.

topics.name: Subtraction Related to Addition Facts
topics.description: Use known addition facts to solve subtraction (e.g., 15-8=? because 8+7=15).

topics.name: Subtract Two-Digit Numbers (No Regrouping)
topics.description: Subtract two-digit numbers without regrouping (e.g., 67 - 23).

topics.name: Subtract Two-Digit Numbers (With Regrouping)
topics.description: Subtract two-digit numbers with regrouping (borrowing) from the tens place.

topics.name: Subtract Three-Digit Numbers
topics.description: Subtract three-digit numbers using the standard algorithm with regrouping.

topics.name: Subtract Across Zeros
topics.description: Subtract from numbers with zeros in the middle or end (e.g., 400 - 167).

topics.name: Subtract Multi-Digit Numbers (4+ digits)
topics.description: Subtract numbers with four or more digits using the standard algorithm.

topics.name: Estimate Differences
topics.description: Use rounding and compatible numbers to estimate differences.

topics.name: Subtraction Word Problems: Take-Away/Separate
topics.description: Solve word problems where a quantity is taken away from a starting amount.

topics.name: Subtraction Word Problems: Compare
topics.description: Solve word problems involving comparison using subtraction.

topics.name: Subtraction Word Problems: Missing Part
topics.description: Solve word problems where the subtrahend or difference is unknown.

topics.name: Mental Subtraction Strategies
topics.description: Use mental strategies like counting up, compensation, and place-value splitting to subtract.

topics.name: Subtraction on a Number Line
topics.description: Represent and solve subtraction problems by making jumps backward on a number line.

topics.name: Check Subtraction Using Addition
topics.description: Verify subtraction answers by adding the difference and subtrahend.

topics.name: Multi-Step Subtraction Word Problems
topics.description: Solve word problems requiring two or more subtraction steps or mixed addition/subtraction.

topics.name: Equal Groups (Introduction to Multiplication)
topics.description: Understand multiplication as combining equal groups of objects.

topics.name: Multiply by 2, 5, and 10
topics.description: Multiply using anchor facts: 2s, 5s, and 10s.

topics.name: Multiply by 0 and 1 (Identity and Zero Properties)
topics.description: Understand that any number times 0 is 0 and any number times 1 is itself.

topics.name: Multiply by 3 and 4
topics.description: Learn multiplication facts for 3s and 4s.

topics.name: Multiply by 6, 7, 8, and 9
topics.description: Learn the remaining single-digit multiplication facts.

topics.name: Multiplication Fluency (Within 100)
topics.description: Fluently multiply single-digit numbers with speed and accuracy.

topics.name: Multiply by 10, 100, and 1,000
topics.description: Multiply whole numbers by 10, 100, and 1,000 using place-value patterns.

topics.name: Multiply Multiples of 10
topics.description: Multiply multiples of 10 (e.g., 30 × 40 = 1,200) using place-value reasoning.

topics.name: Multiply 2-Digit by 1-Digit (No Regrouping)
topics.description: Multiply a two-digit number by a one-digit number without regrouping (e.g., 23 × 3).

topics.name: Multiply 2-Digit by 1-Digit (With Regrouping)
topics.description: Multiply a two-digit number by a one-digit number with regrouping (e.g., 47 × 6).

topics.name: Multiply 3-Digit by 1-Digit
topics.description: Multiply a three-digit number by a one-digit number using the standard algorithm.

topics.name: Multiply 4+-Digit by 1-Digit
topics.description: Multiply a number with four or more digits by a one-digit number.

topics.name: Multiply 2-Digit by 2-Digit (Area Model)
topics.description: Multiply two two-digit numbers using the area model (box method).

topics.name: Multiply 2-Digit by 2-Digit (Standard Algorithm)
topics.description: Multiply two two-digit numbers using the standard algorithm with partial products.

topics.name: Multiply Multi-Digit by 2-Digit
topics.description: Multiply a multi-digit number by a two-digit number.

topics.name: Multiply 3-Digit by 3-Digit
topics.description: Multiply two three-digit numbers using the standard algorithm.

topics.name: Estimate Products
topics.description: Use rounding and compatible numbers to estimate products.

topics.name: Multiplication Word Problems: Equal Groups
topics.description: Solve word problems involving equal groups using multiplication.

topics.name: Multiplication Word Problems: Multiplicative Comparison
topics.description: Solve word problems involving multiplicative comparison (e.g., 3 times as many).

topics.name: Multiplication Word Problems: Area
topics.description: Solve word problems involving area using multiplication.

topics.name: Relationship Between Multiplication and Division
topics.description: Understand that multiplication and division are inverse operations and use fact families.

topics.name: Commutative Property of Multiplication
topics.description: Understand and apply the commutative property: a × b = b × a.

topics.name: Associative and Distributive Properties
topics.description: Understand and apply associative and distributive properties of multiplication.

topics.name: Division as Equal Sharing/Grouping
topics.description: Understand division as splitting into equal groups or finding how many groups.

topics.name: Divide by 2, 5, and 10
topics.description: Divide using anchor divisors: 2, 5, and 10.

topics.name: Divide by 3 and 4
topics.description: Divide by 3 and 4 using related multiplication facts.

topics.name: Divide by 6 through 9
topics.description: Divide by 6, 7, 8, and 9 using known multiplication facts.

topics.name: Dividends, Divisors, Quotients, and Remainders
topics.description: Identify and use correct vocabulary for division components.

topics.name: Division with Remainders (Introduction)
topics.description: Understand remainders and find quotients with remainders for single-digit divisors.

topics.name: Interpret Remainders in Context
topics.description: Decide what to do with a remainder based on the problem context (round up, drop, or express as fraction).

topics.name: Divide by 10, 100, and 1,000
topics.description: Divide whole numbers by 10, 100, and 1,000 using place-value patterns.

topics.name: Estimate Quotients
topics.description: Use compatible numbers and rounding to estimate quotients.

topics.name: Long Division by 1-Digit (No Remainder)
topics.description: Divide multi-digit numbers by a one-digit divisor with no remainder using the standard algorithm.

topics.name: Long Division by 1-Digit (With Remainder)
topics.description: Divide multi-digit numbers by a one-digit divisor with a remainder using the standard algorithm.

topics.name: Long Division by 2-Digit Divisor
topics.description: Divide multi-digit numbers by a two-digit divisor using the standard algorithm.

topics.name: Long Division (Larger Numbers)
topics.description: Divide larger multi-digit numbers using long division with multi-step regrouping.

topics.name: Division with Zeros in Quotient
topics.description: Handle zeros in the quotient when dividing (e.g., 612 ÷ 6 = 102).

topics.name: Fractions as Division
topics.description: Understand that a/b means a ÷ b and express division results as fractions.

topics.name: Division Word Problems: Equal Sharing
topics.description: Solve word problems where items are shared equally among groups.

topics.name: Division Word Problems: How Many Groups
topics.description: Solve word problems asking how many groups can be formed from a total.

topics.name: Multi-Step Division Word Problems
topics.description: Solve word problems requiring division combined with other operations.

topics.name: Division Word Problems: Interpret Remainder
topics.description: Solve word problems where the remainder must be interpreted in context.

topics.name: Divisibility Rules for 2, 5, 10
topics.description: Determine if a number is divisible by 2, 5, or 10 using divisibility rules.

topics.name: Divisibility Rules for 3, 6, 9
topics.description: Determine if a number is divisible by 3, 6, or 9 using digit-sum rules.

topics.name: Count by 2s
topics.description: Skip count by 2s to at least 20.

topics.name: Count by 5s
topics.description: Skip count by 5s to at least 100.

topics.name: Count by 10s
topics.description: Skip count by 10s to 100.

topics.name: Place Value: Thousands
topics.description: Understand place value for numbers up to 9,999 including the thousands place.

topics.name: Place Value: Millions (Large Numbers)
topics.description: Read, write, and understand place value for numbers through millions.

topics.name: Read and Write Numbers in Words
topics.description: Read and write multi-digit numbers using base-ten numerals, number names, and expanded form.

topics.name: Standard Form and Expanded Form
topics.description: Write numbers in standard form and expanded form (e.g., 3,456 = 3,000 + 400 + 50 + 6).

topics.name: Compare Multi-Digit Whole Numbers
topics.description: Compare multi-digit whole numbers using >, <, = based on place value.

topics.name: Round to Nearest 1,000 and Beyond
topics.description: Round numbers to the nearest thousand, ten thousand, and hundred thousand.

topics.name: Equivalent Place Value Representations
topics.description: Recognize that the same number can be represented with different place value groupings (e.g., 24 tens = 240).

topics.name: Decimal Place Value: Tenths
topics.description: Understand tenths as a fractional part and represent them as decimals.

topics.name: Decimal Place Value: Hundredths
topics.description: Understand hundredths and represent them as decimals.

topics.name: Decimal Place Value: Thousandths
topics.description: Understand thousandths place and read/write decimals to thousandths.

topics.name: Read and Write Decimals in Words
topics.description: Read and write decimals to thousandths using base-ten numerals, number names, and expanded form.

topics.name: Equivalent Decimals
topics.description: Recognize that 0.5 = 0.50 = 0.500 and explain why using place value.

topics.name: Standard and Expanded Form with Decimals
topics.description: Write decimals in standard form and expanded form using fractions or decimals.

topics.name: Add Decimals
topics.description: Add decimals to hundredths using place value strategies and algorithms.

topics.name: Subtract Decimals
topics.description: Subtract decimals to hundredths using place value strategies and algorithms.

topics.name: Add Decimals with Unequal Decimal Places
topics.description: Add decimals where the addends have different numbers of decimal places (e.g., 3.4 + 2.15).

topics.name: Subtract Decimals with Unequal Decimal Places
topics.description: Subtract decimals where the numbers have different numbers of decimal places.

topics.name: Multiply Decimals by Whole Numbers
topics.description: Multiply a decimal number by a whole number using place value understanding.

topics.name: Multiply Decimals by Decimals
topics.description: Multiply two decimal numbers and place the decimal point correctly in the product.

topics.name: Divide Decimals by Whole Numbers
topics.description: Divide a decimal number by a whole number.

topics.name: Divide Decimals by Decimals
topics.description: Divide a decimal by a decimal by converting the divisor to a whole number.

topics.name: Multiply Decimals by Powers of Ten
topics.description: Use patterns to multiply decimals by 10, 100, and 1,000.

topics.name: Divide Decimals by Powers of Ten
topics.description: Use patterns to divide decimals by 10, 100, and 1,000.

topics.name: Estimate Decimal Sums, Differences, Products
topics.description: Use rounding and estimation to check reasonableness of decimal computations.

topics.name: Decimal Division with Rounding
topics.description: Divide decimals and round the quotient to a specified place.

topics.name: Factor Pairs
topics.description: Find all factor pairs for a whole number.

topics.name: Multiples of a Number
topics.description: Determine whether a given whole number is a multiple of a given one-digit number.

topics.name: Prime Factorization
topics.description: Find the prime factorization of a number using factor trees.

topics.name: GCF Using Prime Factorization
topics.description: Find the greatest common factor using prime factorization.

topics.name: LCM Using Prime Factorization
topics.description: Find the least common multiple using prime factorization.

topics.name: Divisibility Rules (All)
topics.description: Apply divisibility rules for 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, and 10.

topics.name: Introduction to Negative Numbers
topics.description: Understand negative numbers as values below zero on a number line.

topics.name: Compare Negative Numbers
topics.description: Compare and order negative numbers using a number line.

topics.name: Unit Fractions
topics.description: Understand unit fractions (1/b) as one part of a whole divided into b equal parts.

topics.name: Fractions of a Set
topics.description: Find a fraction of a set of objects (e.g., 1/3 of 12 objects).

topics.name: Read and Write Fractions in Words
topics.description: Read and write fractions using words (e.g., three fourths).

topics.name: Equal Parts and Fractions
topics.description: Partition shapes and sets into equal parts and name the parts using fractions.

topics.name: Equivalent Fractions Using Models
topics.description: Use visual models (area models, number lines) to recognize and generate equivalent fractions.

topics.name: Simplify Fractions (Lowest Terms)
topics.description: Simplify fractions to lowest terms by dividing numerator and denominator by their GCF.

topics.name: Least Common Denominator
topics.description: Find the least common denominator of two fractions.

topics.name: Compare Fractions Using Benchmarks
topics.description: Compare fractions to benchmark values 0, 1/2, and 1 to determine relative size.

topics.name: Order Fractions on a Number Line
topics.description: Place and order fractions on a number line.

topics.name: Decompose Fractions into Sums
topics.description: Write a fraction as a sum of fractions with the same denominator (e.g., 3/4 = 1/4 + 1/4 + 1/4).

topics.name: Add Fractions with Like Denominators
topics.description: Add two fractions that have the same denominator.

topics.name: Subtract Fractions with Like Denominators
topics.description: Subtract fractions that have the same denominator.

topics.name: Add/Subtract Fractions Word Problems (Like Denom)
topics.description: Solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions with like denominators.

topics.name: Add Mixed Numbers with Like Denominators
topics.description: Add mixed numbers that have the same denominator.

topics.name: Subtract Mixed Numbers with Like Denominators
topics.description: Subtract mixed numbers that have the same denominator.

topics.name: Subtract Mixed Numbers (Regrouping)
topics.description: Subtract mixed numbers that require regrouping from the whole number part.

topics.name: Add Fractions with Unlike Denominators
topics.description: Add fractions with different denominators by finding a common denominator.

topics.name: Subtract Fractions with Unlike Denominators
topics.description: Subtract fractions with different denominators by finding a common denominator.

topics.name: Estimate Fraction Sums and Differences
topics.description: Use benchmark fractions to estimate sums and differences of fractions.

topics.name: Add Fractions and Whole Numbers
topics.description: Add a fraction to a whole number or a whole number to a fraction.

topics.name: Subtract Fractions from Whole Numbers
topics.description: Subtract a fraction from a whole number.

topics.name: Add Mixed Numbers with Unlike Denominators
topics.description: Add mixed numbers with different denominators.

topics.name: Subtract Mixed Numbers with Unlike Denominators
topics.description: Subtract mixed numbers with different denominators, including regrouping.

topics.name: Add/Subtract Fractions Word Problems (Unlike Denom)
topics.description: Solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions with unlike denominators.

topics.name: Mixed Number Word Problems
topics.description: Solve word problems involving addition, subtraction, and multiplication of mixed numbers.

topics.name: Multiply Unit Fraction by Whole Number
topics.description: Multiply a unit fraction by a whole number (e.g., 4 × 1/3).

topics.name: Multiply Fractions by Whole Numbers: Word Problems
topics.description: Solve word problems involving multiplication of a fraction by a whole number.

topics.name: Multiply Fractions (Procedural)
topics.description: Multiply two fractions by multiplying numerators and denominators.

topics.name: Multiply Fractions: Word Problems
topics.description: Solve word problems involving multiplication of fractions.

topics.name: Fraction Multiplication and Area
topics.description: Use fraction multiplication to find areas of rectangles with fractional side lengths.

topics.name: Multiply Mixed Numbers by Whole Numbers
topics.description: Multiply a mixed number by a whole number.

topics.name: Multiply Mixed Numbers by Fractions
topics.description: Multiply a mixed number by a fraction.

topics.name: Multiply Mixed Numbers: Word Problems
topics.description: Solve word problems involving multiplication of mixed numbers.

topics.name: Reciprocals
topics.description: Find the reciprocal of a fraction and understand that a number times its reciprocal equals 1.

topics.name: Divide Whole Number by Unit Fraction
topics.description: Divide a whole number by a unit fraction (e.g., 6 ÷ 1/3).

topics.name: Divide Unit Fraction by Whole Number
topics.description: Divide a unit fraction by a whole number (e.g., 1/4 ÷ 3).

topics.name: Divide Whole Number by Fraction
topics.description: Divide a whole number by any fraction using the reciprocal.

topics.name: Divide Fraction by Whole Number
topics.description: Divide any fraction by a whole number.

topics.name: Divide Fractions (Procedural)
topics.description: Divide fractions using the invert-and-multiply method.

topics.name: Divide Fractions and Whole Numbers: Word Problems
topics.description: Solve word problems involving division of fractions and whole numbers.

topics.name: Convert Fractions to Decimals
topics.description: Convert fractions to decimals by dividing numerator by denominator.

topics.name: Convert Decimals to Fractions
topics.description: Convert decimals to fractions and simplify.

topics.name: Convert Mixed Numbers to Decimals
topics.description: Convert mixed numbers to decimal form.

topics.name: Fractions as Division
topics.description: Understand that a/b means a divided by b.

topics.name: Decimal Fractions
topics.description: Express fractions with denominators 10 and 100 as decimals.

topics.name: Simplify Before Multiplying Fractions
topics.description: Cross-cancel common factors before multiplying fractions to simplify computation.

topics.name: Cross-Multiplication for Comparing Fractions
topics.description: Use cross-multiplication as a strategy for comparing two fractions.

topics.name: Convert Between Improper Fractions and Mixed Numbers
topics.description: Fluently convert between improper fractions and mixed numbers in both directions.

topics.name: Fraction Number Sense
topics.description: Determine reasonableness of fraction computations using estimation and benchmarks.

topics.name: Input-Output Tables
topics.description: Complete input-output tables and identify the rule.

topics.name: Growing Patterns (Visual and Numeric)
topics.description: Identify and extend growing patterns using shapes and numbers.

topics.name: Describe Number Rules
topics.description: Generate and describe number patterns following a given rule.

topics.name: Additive Comparison Word Problems
topics.description: Solve word problems using additive comparison (how many more/fewer).

topics.name: Multiplicative Comparison Word Problems
topics.description: Solve word problems using multiplicative comparison (times as many).

topics.name: Evaluate Expressions with Four Operations
topics.description: Evaluate numerical expressions involving all four operations.

topics.name: Multi-Step Word Problems with Four Operations
topics.description: Solve multi-step word problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division.

topics.name: Write Numerical Expressions from Words
topics.description: Translate verbal phrases into numerical expressions.

topics.name: Evaluate Expressions with Grouping Symbols
topics.description: Evaluate expressions containing parentheses, brackets, or braces.

topics.name: Introduction to Algebraic Expressions
topics.description: Understand that letters represent unknown quantities in expressions.

topics.name: Construct Algebraic Expressions from Words
topics.description: Write algebraic expressions from verbal descriptions.

topics.name: Evaluate Algebraic Expressions
topics.description: Substitute values for variables and evaluate the expression.

topics.name: Identify Terms, Coefficients, and Constants
topics.description: Identify the parts of an algebraic expression: terms, coefficients, and constants.

topics.name: Identify and Collect Like Terms
topics.description: Identify like terms and combine them to simplify expressions.

topics.name: Distributive Property (Introduction)
topics.description: Apply the distributive property to rewrite expressions (e.g., 3 × (4 + 2) = 3 × 4 + 3 × 2).

topics.name: Simplify Expressions Using Distributive Property
topics.description: Use the distributive property and combining like terms to simplify expressions.

topics.name: Factor Numerical Expressions
topics.description: Factor numerical expressions using the GCF (e.g., 36 + 24 = 12(3 + 2)).

topics.name: Evaluate Expressions with Exponents
topics.description: Evaluate numerical expressions that include exponents.

topics.name: Two-Step Word Problems
topics.description: Solve word problems requiring exactly two steps.

topics.name: Measure Length in Inches
topics.description: Measure the length of an object in inches using a ruler.

topics.name: Measure Length in Centimeters
topics.description: Measure the length of an object in centimeters using a ruler.

topics.name: Measure Length Using Rulers (Half/Quarter Inch)
topics.description: Measure objects to the nearest half inch and quarter inch.

topics.name: Weight and Mass (Introduction)
topics.description: Compare and measure weight using non-standard and standard units.

topics.name: Capacity and Volume (Liquid Measurement)
topics.description: Compare and measure liquid volumes using standard units.

topics.name: Tell Time to 5 Minutes
topics.description: Tell and write time to the nearest 5 minutes using analog and digital clocks.

topics.name: Elapsed Time
topics.description: Calculate elapsed time in hours and minutes.

topics.name: Picture Graphs
topics.description: Read and interpret picture graphs with single-unit and multi-unit scales.

topics.name: Read and Interpret Bar Graphs
topics.description: Read bar graphs and answer questions about the data.

topics.name: Create Bar Graphs
topics.description: Organize data and create bar graphs with appropriate scales and labels.

topics.name: Line Plots (Introduction)
topics.description: Create and interpret line plots with whole-number data.

topics.name: Line Plots with Fractions
topics.description: Create and interpret line plots with fractional data (halves, quarters, eighths).

topics.name: Units of Length (Customary)
topics.description: Know and use relationships among inches, feet, yards, and miles.

topics.name: Units of Length (Metric)
topics.description: Know and use relationships among millimeters, centimeters, meters, and kilometers.

topics.name: Units of Mass
topics.description: Know and convert among ounces, pounds, grams, and kilograms.

topics.name: Units of Time
topics.description: Know and convert among seconds, minutes, hours, days, weeks, and years.

topics.name: Units of Volume (Liquid)
topics.description: Know and convert among cups, pints, quarts, gallons, milliliters, and liters.

topics.name: Convert Length to Smaller Units
topics.description: Convert larger length units to smaller units (e.g., feet to inches).

topics.name: Convert Length to Larger Units
topics.description: Convert smaller length units to larger units (e.g., inches to feet) including fractions/decimals.

topics.name: Convert Units of Mass
topics.description: Convert between units of mass within a system.

topics.name: Convert Units of Time
topics.description: Convert between units of time (e.g., hours to minutes, days to hours).

topics.name: Convert Units of Volume
topics.description: Convert between units of liquid volume within a system.

topics.name: Compare Metric and Customary Units
topics.description: Compare corresponding units across metric and customary systems.

topics.name: Make Change
topics.description: Determine the correct change when paying for items.

topics.name: Money Word Problems
topics.description: Solve word problems involving dollars and cents using addition, subtraction, and multiplication.

topics.name: Read and Create Data Tables
topics.description: Read, interpret, and create data tables to organize information.

topics.name: Describe Shape Attributes
topics.description: Describe 2D shapes by their attributes (number of sides, corners, straight/curved).

topics.name: Compose and Decompose Shapes
topics.description: Combine simple shapes to form larger shapes and break shapes into smaller parts.

topics.name: Partition Shapes into Equal Parts
topics.description: Partition circles and rectangles into two and four equal parts and describe the parts.

topics.name: Count Sides and Vertices
topics.description: Count the sides and vertices of 2D shapes.

topics.name: Quadrilaterals (Introduction)
topics.description: Identify and classify quadrilaterals by their properties.

topics.name: Rectangles, Rhombuses, and Squares
topics.description: Understand properties of rectangles, rhombuses, and squares and their relationships.

topics.name: Classify Quadrilaterals (Hierarchy)
topics.description: Classify quadrilaterals in a hierarchy based on properties (parallelogram, rectangle, rhombus, square, trapezoid).

topics.name: Polygons (Introduction)
topics.description: Identify polygons, name them by number of sides, and distinguish from non-polygons.

topics.name: Regular Polygons
topics.description: Identify regular polygons and understand that all sides and angles are equal.

topics.name: Measuring Angles with a Protractor
topics.description: Measure angles in degrees using a protractor.

topics.name: Types of Angles
topics.description: Classify angles as acute, obtuse, right, straight, or reflex.

topics.name: Complementary Angles
topics.description: Identify complementary angles (sum to 90°) and find missing angle measures.

topics.name: Supplementary Angles
topics.description: Identify supplementary angles (sum to 180°) and find missing angle measures.

topics.name: Sums of Angles
topics.description: Use the additive property of angle measure to find unknown angles.

topics.name: Solve Angle Problems
topics.description: Solve multi-step problems involving angle relationships.

topics.name: Perimeter of Regular Shapes
topics.description: Find the perimeter of regular shapes using multiplication.

topics.name: Perimeter of Irregular Shapes
topics.description: Find the perimeter of irregular shapes by adding all side lengths.

topics.name: Area by Counting Unit Squares
topics.description: Find the area of a shape by counting unit squares.

topics.name: Area of Rectangles
topics.description: Find the area of rectangles using the formula length × width.

topics.name: Relate Area and Perimeter
topics.description: Understand that shapes with the same area can have different perimeters and vice versa.

topics.name: Congruent Figures
topics.description: Identify congruent figures and understand that congruent shapes have the same size and shape.

topics.name: Volume Using Unit Cubes
topics.description: Find volume by counting unit cubes.

topics.name: Volume of Rectangular Prisms (Formula)
topics.description: Find volume of rectangular prisms using V = l × w × h.

topics.name: Volume Word Problems
topics.description: Solve word problems involving volume of rectangular prisms.

topics.name: Plot Points on the Coordinate Plane
topics.description: Plot ordered pairs on the first-quadrant coordinate plane.

topics.name: Read Points from the Coordinate Plane
topics.description: Read and identify coordinates of points on a coordinate plane.

topics.name: Real World Problems Using Coordinates
topics.description: Solve real-world problems by graphing points on the coordinate plane.

topics.name: Rotational Symmetry
topics.description: Identify rotational symmetry and the order of rotational symmetry.

topics.name: Classify Triangles by Sides
topics.description: Classify triangles as equilateral, isosceles, or scalene based on side lengths.

topics.name: Classify Triangles by Angles
topics.description: Classify triangles as acute, right, or obtuse based on their angles.

topics.name: Draw Geometric Shapes with Given Attributes
topics.description: Draw shapes with specified attributes (e.g., parallel sides, specific angle measures).

topics.name: 3D Shape Attributes
topics.description: Identify faces, edges, and vertices of 3D shapes.

topics.name: Nets of 3D Shapes
topics.description: Identify nets that fold into 3D shapes.

topics.name: Units of Area
topics.description: Understand and convert between units of area (sq in, sq ft, sq cm, sq m).

topics.name: Area and Perimeter Word Problems
topics.description: Solve word problems involving area and perimeter of rectangles.

topics.name: Modeling with Rectangles
topics.description: Use rectangles to model and solve real-world problems involving area.
